summary:
Avelumab effectively treats bladder cancer by blocking PD-1 signaling, leading to enhanced anti-tumor immunity. Clinical trial data show significant improvement in overall survival among patients with advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma, which encompasses bladder cancer. 

The summary above contains redundant information about mechanism and disease equivalence; please revise strictly per instructions — only two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically meaningful outcomes related directly to efficacy in bladder cancer.

**Avelumab demonstrates improved overall survival in patients with metastatic urothelial carcinoma, including those with bladder cancer. It is FDA-approved and supported by multiple clinical studies confirming its therapeutic benefit in this indication.**
